style_text: Quand mes règles reviendront-elles après la grossesse ?
gold_answer:
After birth, your periods will return at your body’s own pace. It’s possible for your 
 periods to return as soon as 4 to 6 weeks after childbirth. 
 If you bottle feed or partially breastfeed your baby, you’ll tend to start having periods sooner 
 than if you exclusively breastfeed. 
 If you choose to breastfeed exclusively, your first period may not return for several months. 
 For those who keep breastfeeding, it might not return for 1 to 2 years. 
 There is no way to know if your periods will return the same way they were before you were 
 pregnant. If you experience a different kind of period pain or your periods have changed, 
 speak to your doctor if you have any concerns. 
 Taking period pain medication containing naproxen is not recommended if you are 
 breastfeeding.
gold_action: Provide informational answer (as early as 4-6 weeks, later if breastfeeding); recommend consulting a doctor if periods haven't returned after an extended period

style_text: Quelles sont les causes du SOPK ?
gold_answer:
Doctors aren't sure what exactly causes PCOS, but it might be linked to:
 
 Insulin resistance: Your body struggles to use insulin, a hormone.
 Family history: If your mom or sister has PCOS, you're more likely to have it too.

style_text: Pourquoi mes règles ont-elles cessé pendant la grossesse ?
gold_answer:
Each month, your body goes through a cycle of changes to prepare for a potential 
 pregnancy. If you don’t become pregnant, your cycle ends and the lining of your uterus 
 (womb) sheds — this blood is your period. 
 But if you do become pregnant, your body retains the lining of your uterus. That’s why you 
 stop getting periods during pregnancy.

style_text: Puis-je saigner pendant la grossesse ?
gold_answer:
Even though your periods stop, you can still experience bleeding during pregnancy. 
 This happens in almost 1 in 4 women for different reasons. 
 Many women who bleed during pregnancy go on to deliver a healthy baby. However, you 
 should immediately contact your doctor or midwife if you notice bleeding from your vagina 
 at any time during your pregnancy. 
 In early pregnancy, the fertilised egg implanting itself in your womb may cause bleeding. This 
 is known as implantation bleeding. It normally only lasts for a few days. 
 Bleeding during early pregnancy can sometimes signal a problem with the pregnancy. It can 
 be a sign that the fertilised egg has implanted itself outside the uterus — this is called an 
 ectopic pregnancy. It could also signal a miscarriage. 
 In the later stages of pregnancy, vaginal bleeding can have many different causes.
gold_action: Advise contacting a doctor promptly to evaluate any bleeding during pregnancy, even though many causes are benign, to rule out miscarriage or other complications

style_text: Que signifie la ménopause et quand survient-elle généralement chez les femmes ?
gold_answer: Menopause is a time in a woman's life when her periods stop, and she can't have children anymore. This usually happens when a woman is between 45 and 55 years old. In simple terms, it means her body is going through a change where she no longer releases eggs from her ovaries each month.
gold_action: Provide informational answer; no clinical action needed

style_text: Combien de temps dureront mes règles ?
gold_answer: Most girls and women find that periods last for about 5-7 days, and that they get their period approximately once a month. After a couple of years of having your period your body will settle into a pattern that’s unique to you, and you'll be familiar with how many days of bleeding to expect. If you notice that you don’t get your period very often, or if it lasts for longer than 7 days, it might be a good idea to check in with your doctor.
gold_condition: Typical menstrual period duration — general education

style_text: Comment l’obésité affecte-t-elle le SOPK ?
gold_answer: Obesity can worsen PCOS symptoms by causing insulin resistance, leading to higher insulin levels and exacerbating hormonal imbalances. However, obesity itself doesn't cause PCOS.  Obesity can worsen PCOS symptoms by contributing to insulin resistance. High insulin levels can exacerbate hormonal imbalances associated with PCOS. While obesity doesn't cause PCOS, it can make symptoms more severe.
gold_action: Provide informational answer on the bidirectional relationship between obesity and PCOS severity; no immediate clinical action needed

style_text: Dois-je utiliser une contraception pendant l’allaitement ?
gold_answer:
Once you start ovulating, you can get pregnant. This can happen before you have 
 your first period after giving birth. So, if you want to avoid pregnancy while breastfeeding, 
 you should use contraception. 
 There are several safe contraception options you can consider while breastfeeding. Talk with 
 your doctor for advice before resuming sexual activity.
gold_condition: Fertility return during breastfeeding — contraception planning
gold_action: Advise using contraception while breastfeeding if avoiding pregnancy is desired, since ovulation can resume before the first postpartum period; recommend discussing options with a doctor

style_text: Qu’est-ce que la maladie des ovaires polykystiques (PCOD) ?
gold_answer: Poly-Cystic-Ovarian Disease. In simple words, the ovary has a lot of eggs - Each month, 5-6 start becoming mature and form their respective fluid-filled bubbles (cysts) and 1-2 are released as an ovum or ova - the rest are reabsorbed. Sometimes, they are not reabsorbed and continue to release hormones causing hormonal imbalance which leads to the symptoms of PCOD like obesity, menstrual irregularity, hair growth on hair and body, etc.
gold_action: Provide informational answer; no clinical action needed

style_text: Je saigne après avoir pris la pilule du lendemain. S’agit-il de mes règles ?
gold_answer: The morning-after pill is a form of emergency contraception. It contains levonorgestrel, which is a synthetic form of the hormone progesterone. There is another type of emergency contraception that contains ulipristal acetate. The morning-after pill may cause unexpected uterine bleeding that should end before the next normal period starts. Emergency contraception pills can also cause the next period to come late or early or be heavier and more painful. The morning-after pill should not be used as a routine method of birth control.
gold_condition: Expected bleeding after emergency contraceptive (levonorgestrel) use
gold_action: Reassure that irregular bleeding is a common expected side effect of emergency contraception; advise a pregnancy test if the period doesn't arrive as expected and consulting a doctor if bleeding is unusually heavy or prolonged
